channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Houthis deploy missiles and drones to attack ships in southern Red Sea, naval group says
item.description: Attacks on ships transiting the Bab el-Mandeb threaten to cut off millions of barrels per day of Saudi crude oil exports.

item.title: OpenAI cyber models broke out of training environment to hack Hugging Face
item.description: The incident is unique because it was "driven, end to end, by an autonomous AI agent system," according to Hugging Face.

item.title: Senate crypto bill would ban federal officials — including presidents — from issuing digital assets
item.description: A new version of the Clarity Act would impose the first limits on federal officials sponsoring or issuing cryptocurrency.

item.title: S&P 500 ticks higher as traders monitor rising oil prices, gear up for more earnings: Live updates
item.description: Investors looked ahead to another busy day of corporate earnings, with several major technology and industrial companies set to report.

item.title: Congressional stock trading curb due to get House vote amid public support for a ban
item.description: The House is scheduled to vote on Wednesday on a bill that would bar members of Congress from purchasing additional stocks while in office.

item.title: Google earnings live updates: Alphabet's AI outlook, cloud growth in focus
item.description: Google parent Alphabet is reporting its second-quarter earning results after the market closes. The conference call with analysts is scheduled for 4:30 p.m. ET.

item.title: Under-the-radar beverage name captures trader's attention
item.description: Michael Khouw breaks down how he's trading this stock ahead of earnings.

item.title: Goldman says the M&A boom has room to run, flags new takeover candidates
item.description: Deal activity has increased sequentially in every month this year, fueled by easy financial conditions and steady economic growth, the firm said.

item.title: Kevin Warsh has homed in on three key phrases. How Fed watchers interpret them
item.description: In five public appearances, Warsh has used the phrase "family fight" 13 times, returning to "first principles" 11 times and "inflation is a choice" for the Fed six times.

item.title: Mortgage rates are rising again, but homebuyers are seeing some advantages
item.description: Mortgage rates last week rose to the highest level since last August, but homebuyer demand climbed as well, as buyers see more supply.

item.title: $100M New Jersey deli fraudster James Patten sentenced to 21 months in prison
item.description: The notorious New Jersey deli fraud case led to losses of more than $5 million for victims that included two U.S. universities.

item.title: Renting a yacht in the Mediterranean this summer just got cheaper
item.description: Summer charters in the Med are down 20% to 30% from last year, yacht brokers say, with a growing number offering special discounts for July and August.

item.title: Reddit stock sinks on report it may not renew Google AI content deal
item.description: Google's AI summaries have reduced traffic to websites from the search results page and Reddit is reconsidering the benefits of the deal, the Journal said.

item.title: Sen. Wyden introduces bill to curb Trump's tariff powers
item.description: Sen. Ron Wyden said Trump has "abused every trade authority at his disposal" and called his new tariffs on Canadian goods a "shakedown."

item.title: Samsung ups prices as it launches new foldable smartphones ahead of expected Apple debut
item.description: Samsung launched the Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra, Galaxy Z Fold8 and Galaxy Z Flip8.

item.title: Hold or sell Nvidia is a popular question, but there's a better one investors should ask
item.description: Inside Edge Capital's Todd Gordon ponders the question of whether investors should sell-off their Nvidia stock after the chipmaker's historic run.

item.title: AMD to invest up to $5 billion in Anthropic as part of computing power deal
item.description: Anthropic's agreement with AMD is the latest in a string of infrastructure deals the company has announced this year to increase its computing capacity.

item.title: Peacock is getting a lift from World Cup, Spanish-language content
item.description: Peacock attracted new subscribers with the Spanish-language telecast of the World Cup, and executives say advertisers are taking notice.

item.title: Trump's generic drug tariffs: What they could mean for medicine prices, manufacturers and supply
item.description: Trump's generic drug tariff plan raises questions over prices, U.S. manufacturing and which drugmakers stand to gain or lose as details remain unclear.

item.title: Samsung details features of its smart glasses as it enters market dominated by Meta
item.description: The Samsung smart glasses are the tech giant's first foray into the product category which is currently dominated by Meta.

item.title: Jim Cramer's top 10 things to watch in the stock market Wednesday
item.description: The rise in oil prices cannot be ignored today.

item.title: Chase Sapphire Preferred's 100K bonus is ending soon: 4 reasons to apply now
item.description: Chase Sapphire Preferred's 100K bonus is ending soon: Here's how the 100,000 points could be worth roughly $2,000 in travel.

item.title: Trump's push for American-made AI chips hits TSMC's margins
item.description: The Taiwan-based chipmaker has announced $200 billion in investment into U.S. manufacturing since Trump returned to power in 2025.

item.title: Citizens United provision allowing foreign campaign contributions draws Democratic bill
item.description: Rep. Jamie Raskin, D-Md., and Sen. Sheldon Whitehouse, D-R.I., want to establish foreign ownership thresholds for companies that spend on campaigns.

item.title: Kalshi launches election hub for prediction markets ahead of midterms
item.description: The company said the page gives viewers of its event contracts a central place to see speculators' outlook for the midterm elections.

item.title: The Fed rang the alarm about Anthropic's Mythos AI model — but had to go months without it
item.description: The central bank didn't have access to Claude Mythos Preview as of mid-July, even as other institutions were racing to patch their vulnerabilities.

item.title: Nike to cut off thousands of online distributors in China, restructure digital footprint
item.description: Nike is streamlining its online business in China as the company looks to stabilize pricing and branding and get back to growth.

item.title: Fidelity says 2026 retirees may spend $185,500 on healthcare. One category may push those costs higher
item.description: A 65-year-old who retires in 2026 may spend an average of $185,500 on health and medical expenses in retirement, according to Fidelity Investments.

item.title: SpaceX snaps 7-day losing streak, sets earnings date that triggers first big share unlock
item.description: SpaceX's stock gained on Tuesday and looked to snap a seven-day losing streak after announcing Aug. 4 as its maiden earnings report

item.title: OpenAI appoints two new members to board of directors
item.description: OpenAI appointed longtime financial executives David Vélez and Robin Vince to its nonprofit and for-profit boards.
